title: kernelCTF live LTS instance (latest LTS + COS config + restrictions)

boot_smoke.interpretation: historical CVE-2024-1086 exploit failed to trigger KASAN — nftables surface removed

k1_live.rationale: tier1_kasan over live dmesg → no_crash → router maps to inconclusive (no_crash ≠ safe)

k2_historical_positive_control.rationale: tier1_kasan over Phase-0.4 historical dmesg → KASAN UAF at ip_rcv+0x6b1 → confirmed

deferred_to_4.3_or_4.4: syz-manager fuzz pass against the live kernel using oracle/tier1_fuzz/syzlang/nf_tables_cve_2024_1086.txt (now mostly moot — nftables is off; the descriptor will be repointed at a kept-surface subsystem in 4.4)
actual novel-bug hunt (open-ended; Phase 4 Done-when says one PoV on a real target — that can equally come from 4.3 live-lib)

latest LTS upgrade beyond 6.1.x — 6.1.72 is still a current LTS (EOL Dec 2026); moving to 6.6.x or 6.12.x would re-download ~1.4 GB of source and re-build ~6 GB, deferred until 4.4 cost/coverage metrics need a fresh tree
